mnemonics: FLAG: Use FlowLayout, BorderLayout, GridLayout, Absolute (null) for common panel arrangements.
MVC: Model (data), View (Swing component), Controller (Event Listeners) - the Swing design pattern.
EDT: Only the Event Dispatch Thread can update GUI components. Remember 'Don't Touch GUI from outside EDT'.
keyPoints: Swing components are lightweight, written entirely in Java, unlike the heavyweight AWT components.
Layout Managers (like BorderLayout, GridLayout) are crucial for creating responsive and platform-independent GUIs.
Event Handling is done through the Delegation Event Model (registering listeners to components).
commonMistakes: Performing long-running tasks (e.g., database queries) on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT), which freezes the GUI.
Using null layout (absolute positioning) which makes the GUI non-responsive and look bad on different platforms.
Not understanding the different layout managers and nesting panels to achieve the desired design.
